tiers.name: Tier 1 — Flagship Projects (active or near-submission)
tiers.summary: Flagship work close to paper conversion or major validation milestones.
tiers.opportunity: 42.9% explicit status coverage; freeze labels for 4 needs-triage rows.

tiers.name: Tier 2 — HTML Apps Portfolio
tiers.summary: Browser-based evidence synthesis apps with publication-facing companions.
tiers.opportunity: 100.0% explicit status coverage; continue maintaining the current labels.

tiers.name: Tier 3 — MetaSprint Family (C:\Projects\)
tiers.summary: MetaSprint product family spanning automation and multiple evidence modes.
tiers.opportunity: 100.0% explicit status coverage; continue maintaining the current labels.

tiers.name: Tier 4 — R Packages & Methods Papers
tiers.summary: Packages and methods papers with strong manuscript and validation depth.
tiers.opportunity: 100.0% explicit status coverage; continue maintaining the current labels.
tiers.statusSummary: Active 1, Submission ready 8, Accepted 1, Shipped 5, Paused 5

tiers.name: Tier 5 — Cutting-Edge MA Trio + New Root-Level Methods (2026-03-24/25)
tiers.summary: New frontier methods and root-level projects still being sequenced.
tiers.opportunity: 57.1% explicit status coverage; freeze labels for 3 needs-triage rows.

tiers.name: Tier 6 — Infrastructure & Datasets
tiers.summary: Extraction, datasets, and ingestion infrastructure feeding downstream work.
tiers.opportunity: 0.0% explicit status coverage; freeze labels for 8 needs-triage rows.

tiers.name: Tier 7 — HTA Suite
tiers.summary: HTA and value frameworks that sit downstream from synthesis engines.
tiers.opportunity: 100.0% explicit status coverage; continue maintaining the current labels.

tiers.name: Tier 8 — Educational & Course Material
tiers.summary: Educational assets and teaching material rather than production apps.
tiers.opportunity: 0.0% explicit status coverage; freeze labels for 3 needs-triage rows.

tiers.name: Tier 9 — Collaborative & External
tiers.summary: Collaborative or external-facing projects with mixed ownership and timing.
tiers.opportunity: 20.0% explicit status coverage; freeze labels for 4 needs-triage rows.

tiers.name: Tier 10 — Smaller / Standalone Apps
tiers.summary: Standalone apps and methods utilities that widen the portfolio surface.
tiers.opportunity: 3.0% explicit status coverage; freeze labels for 32 needs-triage rows.

tiers.name: Tier 11 — Website & Non-Academic
tiers.summary: Website and non-academic projects outside the core methods lane.
tiers.opportunity: 20.0% explicit status coverage; freeze labels for 4 needs-triage rows.

tiers.name: Tier 12 — Research Projects (C:\Projects\, various)
tiers.summary: Backlog and exploratory research projects requiring clearer prioritisation.
tiers.opportunity: 3.8% explicit status coverage; freeze labels for 25 needs-triage rows.

roadmap.title: Phase 0: Snapshot atlas
roadmap.detail: Compile tier and status data from the bundled project-index snapshot so the public atlas is reproducible from the repository alone.

roadmap.title: Phase 1: Status freeze
roadmap.detail: Use the needs-triage queue to finish explicit labels for every tracked project and reduce ambiguous rows before June 30, 2026.

roadmap.title: Phase 2: Folder metadata
roadmap.detail: Merge last-write timestamps, git presence, paper folders, and validation artifacts from the indexed paths into the portfolio layer.

roadmap.title: Phase 3: Submission cockpit
roadmap.detail: Expose submission, release, and validation state in one surface so the atlas becomes an operating console rather than a directory browser.
